title: Warehouse Ventilation Rate
summary: ASHRAE Standard 62.1 recommends 0.3 L/(s·m²) as a design criterion for warehouse ventilation.
evidence_quote: Table 6-1 of Standard 62.1 recommends 0.3 L/(s·m2) of ventilation as a design criterion for warehouse ventilation

title: Air Curtain Effectiveness Range
summary: Air curtains have a range of effectiveness from 60 to 90%, depending on type and application.
evidence_quote: air curtains have a range of effectiveness from 60 to 90%, depending on the type and application.

title: Air Curtain Minimum Velocity Projection
summary: Air curtain unit must have a minimum velocity projection of 2 m/s at the target surface.
evidence_quote: have a minimum velocity projection of 2 m/s at the target surface

title: Perchloric Acid Exhaust System Material
summary: Ducts for perchloric acid exhaust should be constructed of stainless steel with chromium and nickel content not less than 316L, or suitable nonmetallic material.
evidence_quote: Ducts should be constructed of a stainless steel material, with a chromium and nickel content not less than that of 316L stainless steel, or of a suitable nonmetallic material.

title: Perchloric Acid Exhaust System Washdown
summary: Perchloric acid exhaust system must be equipped with an internal water washdown and drainage system.
evidence_quote: the exhaust system must be equipped with an internal water washdown and drainage system

title: Fume Hood Sash Bypass Prevention
summary: Fume hoods with vertically rising sash sections should include provisions around the sash to prevent bypass of ceiling plenum air.
evidence_quote: Fume hoods with vertically rising sash sections should include provisions around the sash to prevent the bypass of ceiling plenum air into the fume hood.

title: Cultural Heritage Loading Dock Pressurization
summary: Loading docks should ideally be positively pressurized, and shared walls with collections zones should be carefully designed.
evidence_quote: Loading docks should ideally be positively pressurized, and shared walls with collections zones should be carefully designed

title: Cultural Heritage Occupancy Density for Storage Zones
summary: Unoccupied storage zones may have 100 m² per person or less.
evidence_quote: unoccupied storage zones may have 100 m2 per person or less.

title: Cultural Heritage Gallery Occupancy Density
summary: Some gallery occupancies are as high as 1 m² per person.
evidence_quote: Some gallery occupancies are as high as 1 m2 per person

title: Cultural Heritage Design Occupancy Assumption
summary: Many engineers design to 2 m² per person because part of the room is never occupied.
evidence_quote: Many engineers design to 2 m2 per person because part of the room is never occupied.

title: Cultural Heritage Envelope Performance Reassessment
summary: Where envelope performance is in question, first reassess environmental goals: consider higher or lower relative humidity ranges to minimize vapor pressure differences.
evidence_quote: Where envelope performance is in question, the first step is to reassess environmental goals: considering either higher or lower relative humidity ranges to minimize differences in vapor pressure

title: Cultural Heritage Microenvironment Alternatives
summary: Alternatives for containment of preservation environments include microenvironments, offsite purpose-built storage, and box-in-box solutions.
evidence_quote: alternatives for containment of preservation environments should be considered, such as Microenvironments (cabinetry, certain housing solutions, small-scale freezing) Offsite, purpose-built storage, which may provide better environmental control with better energy efficiency Limited envelope upgrades in part of the structure, often called box-in-box solutions

title: Cultural Heritage Modulating Dampers Fail Closed
summary: Modulating outdoor air dampers may be set to fail closed to minimize infiltration during interrupted operation.
evidence_quote: modulating outdoor air dampers may be set to fail closed to minimize infiltration

title: Cultural Heritage Latent Capacity Consideration
summary: Adequate latent capacity is often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs, resulting in environments that may maintain temperature but see relative humidity rise beyond safe limits.
evidence_quote: adequate latent capacity is often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs, resulting in environments that may be able to maintain temperature control, but commonly see relative humidity conditions rise beyond safe limits

title: Ceiling/Plenum/Room Attenuation for Mineral Fiber Tile
summary: Attenuation in dB for mineral fiber tile (4.9 kg/m², 16 mm) at 63 Hz is 13 dB.
